migrationId.description: Stable identifier for a single migration “run context.” Use it to correlate logs, metrics, retries, and artifacts across services. Must be non-empty; recommended to be globally unique (e.g., UUID or human-readable slug + timestamp).

dryRun.description: Safety switch. When true, processors should validate inputs, resolve dependencies, and produce plans (and optionally side-effect-free previews), but must not mutate external systems or persist target-state changes.

priority.description: Scheduling hint for queues/executors. Typical uses: pick a priority lane, adjust concurrency tokens, or bump backoff thresholds. Must not change mid-run; treat it as a queue-time attribute.

orchestrator.description: Metadata used by the controller/runner layer (not business logic).

runOwner.description: Human or service principal responsible for the run. Use in audit trails and for “who to page” on failure.

tags.description: Lightweight labels for search/routing/analytics (e.g., ["mongo→crdb","phase:bulk","env:staging"]). Prefer kebab-case or key:value patterns; avoid secrets.

notifyWebhook.description: Optional callback endpoint to receive status events (started/progress/succeeded/failed). Expect authenticated POSTs with a signed payload including migrationId, status, and a monotonic sequence.

connectionUri.description: The full MongoDB connection string (SRV or standard). This is the single source of truth for hosts/ports/topology, auth mechanism, replicaSet name, timeouts, retryReads/writes, etc.

database.description: The logical DB we’re migrating from (namespace for initial snapshot and change streams).

username.description: Principal to use if not in URI.

passwordSecretRef.description: Secret manager reference (K8s secret path, Vault path, etc.).

authenticationDatabase.description: Database where the user was created, most admin users will be in admin).

verifyPeer.description: Whether to verify server certs. Should be true in prod.

caCertSecretRef.description: Secret ref for custom CA bundle if using private PKI.

readConcern.description: Controls consistency level of reads (local, majority, linearizable, available, snapshot).

writeConcern.description: Acks for writes (majority, w:1, w:0, w:N, often with j/wtimeout in URI).

maxDocsPerBatch.description: Application-level chunk size for processing/commit cycles (e.g., read → transform → stage → commit), primary backpressure & memory control for the pipeline.

cursorBatchSize.description: Hints Mongo how many docs to return per getMore. Smaller = more round trips; larger = fewer calls but bigger bursts.

sqlEndpoint.description: Postgres-compatible connection string or components routed through PgBouncer (e.g., postgres://user@pgbouncer-host:6432/dbname or host:port + db in query).

auth.description: Authentication for the database. Use either password auth or client certs.

username.description: Database role to connect as. Required if not provided in sqlEndpoint.

passwordSecretRef.description: Secret reference for the password if using password auth (e.g., k8s secret key or Vault path).

tls.description: TLS options. Enable server verification and (optionally) client cert auth.

verifyPeer.description: Verify server certificate. Should be true in production.

caCertSecretRef.description: Secret reference containing the CA bundle for server verification.

clientCertSecretRef.description: Secret reference to a PEM-encoded client certificate for mTLS (if used).

clientKeySecretRef.description: Secret reference to the PEM-encoded private key for the client certificate (if used).

database.description: Target database name in CockroachDB. Required if not embedded in sqlEndpoint.

defaultSchema.description: Primary schema for final user-facing tables. Typically 'public'.

stagingSchema.description: Durable scratch schema for bulk loads/merges. Preferred over session temp tables when using PgBouncer transaction pooling.

mode.description: Controls whether DDL is only produced or also executed against the target.

schemaConversion.description: Rules to convert MongoDB documents into CockroachDB DDL and table layouts.

defaultStrategy.description: Global strategy for nested objects: 'columnize' flattens fields into columns up to flattenDepth; 'normalize' prefers child tables for nested objects (opt-in via tableOverrides on object paths); 'jsonb' keeps nested content as JSONB columns.

arrayStrategy.description: How to handle arrays: 'child_table' = 1:N child table with FK to parent; 'junction_table' = linking table for many-to-many or arrays of references/IDs; 'jsonb' = store array unparsed as JSONB.

flattenDepth.description: Maximum nesting levels to inline into the base table when defaultStrategy=columnize. 0 = do not inline nested fields; >0 = inline up to this depth; deeper content follows array/map strategies.

ignorePaths.description: Paths to leave entirely under the control of the existing target schema. No columns/tables are generated or altered for these paths. Use dotted notation; arrays use [] or [*].

typeMap.description: Global BSON→CRDB type overrides. Keys are logical source types or detectors (e.g., 'ObjectId', 'date', 'decimal', 'string'), values are CRDB types (e.g., 'UUID', 'TIMESTAMPTZ', 'DECIMAL(38,9)', 'STRING'). Path-level columnOverrides take precedence.

tableDefaults.description: Defaults for all generated base and child tables unless overridden.

nameStyle.description: Style used when deriving table and column names from Mongo paths.

identifierMaxLen.description: Max length for generated identifiers; names are truncated with stable hashing when exceeded.

id.description: Defaults for synthetic primary keys and preservation of source identifiers.

idStrategy.description: How to form the table PK when no explicit PK is supplied: reuse Mongo _id ('use_objectid'), generate 'UUID', use CRDB 'rowid' (int), or use a 'natural' key from fields.

preserveSourceId.description: Keep the original Mongo _id as a separate column. Usually false when idStrategy='use_objectid' to avoid duplication.

preserveAs.description: Column name used when preserving the original _id.

uniqueIndex.description: Create a UNIQUE index on the preserved source id column.

applyTo.description: Where to apply idStrategy/preservation by default.

arrays.description: Defaults that apply when arrayStrategy produces child/junction tables.

childPkDefault.description: 'synthetic' = generated single-column id per id.idStrategy; 'parent_plus_index' = composite (<parent>_id, arrayIndexColumnName).

arrayIndexColumnName.description: Column name for the array position when childPkDefault=parent_plus_index.

fkOnDelete.description: Default ON DELETE action for FKs from child/junction tables to their parent.

columnDefaults.description: Default column-level typing when no path-specific override is present.

maxVarchar.description: Default upper bound for STRING columns when a fixed length is chosen; larger strings fall back to unbounded STRING.

decimalDefault.description: Default precision/scale for decimal-like values when a more specific mapping is not inferred.

timeTzDefault.description: Default timestamp type when parsing date/time values.

primaryKey.description: Optional default PK shape for base tables if not covered by id.idStrategy.

columns.description: Explicit PK columns (in order). If provided, overrides id.idStrategy for base tables.

descending.description: Subset of PK columns to mark DESCENDING.

shardedBuckets.description: Enable hash-sharded PK with this many buckets (or null for none).

tableOverrides.description: Overrides that apply to a table produced from a path (base collection or columnized/normalized child).

path.description: Dotted Mongo path that becomes/owns this table, e.g., 'orders' (base), 'orders.items[]' (child array), 'orders.shipping' (nested obj). Arrays use [] or [*].

action.description: 'ignore' = do not generate/alter this table (assume it exists and is managed externally).

targetTable.description: Override for the derived table name if you don't want the inferred name.

mode.description: Per-path override for arrays OR objects. For arrays: 'child_table'/'junction_table'/'jsonb'. For nested objects: 'child_table' normalizes the object into a child table; 'jsonb' keeps it as JSONB on the parent.

pk.description: Explicit PK columns for this table (overrides defaults).

fk.description: Foreign key definition if the default parent FK isn't correct or for junction tables.

parentColumns.description: Referenced columns on the parent table.

onDelete.description: Override the default ON DELETE action.

reverse.description: Place the FK on the base table pointing to this normalized table.

childPkPath.description: Field inside the object to use as the child's PK (e.g., 'id')

baseColumnName.description: Name of the FK column to add on the base table (e.g., 'customer_id')

columnFamilies.description: Optional column-family layout for this table.

indexDefs.description: Indexes to add on this table.

name.description: Optional explicit index name; otherwise auto-derived.

type.description: Index method. 'inverted' is typical for JSONB or array containment; 'hash' for hash-sharded secondary idx.

hashShardedBuckets.description: Number of buckets for HASH SHARDED indexes.

columnOverrides.description: Overrides for specific scalar columns produced from paths.

path.description: Dotted path to a scalar column, e.g., 'orders.total', 'orders.items[].sku'.

action.description: 'ignore' = do not generate/alter this column (assume it exists as-is).

crdbName.description: Override for the derived column name.

crdbType.description: Explicit CRDB type override for this column (e.g., 'STRING', 'DECIMAL(38,9)', 'JSONB'). Supersedes typeMap.

nullable.description: Force NULL/NOT NULL regardless of inference.

default.description: DEFAULT expression/value to use when the source value is absent (e.g., '0', 'now()', 'gen_random_uuid()').

expr.description: SQL expression for the computed column.

stored.description: CRDB computed columns are stored; keep true.

collection.description: Per-collection migration settings, describes how to sample, filter, and (optionally) override schema conversion for a specific MongoDB collection.

filters.description: Optional MongoDB query predicate to scope sampling and migration (pass-through to find()). Null means no filter.

sampling.description: Controls how many documents are used for schema inference and example value capture.

size.description: Target number of documents to sample for inference.

strategy.description: Sampling strategy: 'random' across the collection; 'prefix' reads from the beginning; 'time_window' filters by a timestamp field (see filters).

hint.description: Optional index hint string for sampling queries (driver-specific, e.g., '{ field: 1 }' or index name).

fieldTransforms.description: Deterministic, per-path transforms applied during extraction (and thus affecting inference) and/or before write. Useful for coercions, renames, redaction.

path.description: Dotted Mongo path to transform (arrays use []), e.g., 'orders.total', 'orders.items[].sku'.

op.description: Transform operation to apply. See 'args' for per-op parameters.

args.description: Operation-specific parameters. Examples: rename:{to}, cast:{type}, coalesce:{values:[...]}, default_if_missing:{value}, substring:{start,length}, regex_extract:{pattern,group}, concat:{paths:[...],sep}, to_decimal:{precision,scale}, to_timestamp:{format,timezone}, mask:{strategy('hash'|'redact'|'null'),salt}.

phase.description: When to apply the transform. 'both' affects inference and writes; 'inference_only' only affects sampling/inference; 'write_only' only affects outgoing rows.

keys.description: Conflict/upsert semantics. DDL keys are controlled by schemaConversion; this section is for business keys used at load/apply time.

natural.description: Ordered list of fields forming the business key (e.g., ['orderNumber','tenantId']). Used for upsert matching when not using the synthetic PK.

conflicts.description: How to handle key conflicts during initial load vs. CDC apply.

onLoad.description: Behavior during bulk snapshot load: 'fail' on duplicate key; 'upsert' = INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE; 'ignore' = ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING.

onCdc.description: Behavior during change-apply: typically 'upsert'. 'fail' is strict; 'ignore' drops conflicting events.

schemaConversion.description: Optional per-collection overrides that shallow-merge onto the top-level schemaConversion. Use tableOverrides/columnOverrides with paths that start at this collection (e.g., 'orders.items[]').
